key_insight: Uni-XAS unifies forward XAS prediction and inverse structure generation by learning a shared cross-modal alignment space, then using conditional generation to handle the ambiguity of mapping spectra to atomic structures.
engineering_relevance: The framework offers a scalable way to standardize spectroscopy-driven materials inference, supporting more reliable screening, structure reconstruction, and benchmarked multimodal models for materials discovery.
method: XASLip aligns a physics-aware spectral encoder with absorber-aware manifold optimization to capture fine-grained coordination differences.
Forward prediction uses anchored absolute-spectrum generation with retrieval-augmented decoding to reduce scale collapse and energy drift.
Inverse modeling uses Permutation-Rectified Flow Matching with type-wise optimal transport to address ligand permutation ambiguity without heavy equivariant networks.
evidence_signal: The method is evaluated on a standardized benchmark of 328,839 structure-spectrum pairs, with reported gains in cross-modal retrieval, absolute-spectrum prediction, and composition-conditional 3D structure generation.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality and consistency of the alignment recipe and benchmark protocol, and inverse generation remains inherently ill-posed for highly degenerate structures.
paper_title: Uni-XAS: Alignment-Driven Bidirectional Multimodal Learning for X-ray Absorption Spectroscopy
